sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The OptimizerConfig class, implemented in Cython, serves as a configuration container for various optimization parameters within the AnalysisG framework. It provides a structured and efficient way to define and manage settings for different optimizers, leveraging an underlying C++ optimizer_params_t structure for high-performance access and manipulation. This class is crucial for fine-tuning the training process of machine learning models by allowing precise control over optimizer behavior.

sect1.title: The OptimizerConfig Class
sect1.para: OptimizerConfig is a cdef class, indicating its implementation as a Cython extension type. This design choice enables direct and optimized interaction with C++ data structures, which is beneficial for performance-critical applications. It encapsulates a pointer (self.params) to a C++ optimizer_params_t object, managing its lifecycle and exposing its parameters as Python properties.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent, called during object allocation. It initializes a new C++ optimizer_params_t object and assigns its pointer to self.params.
__init__(self): The standard Python initializer, intentionally left empty as primary initialization is handled by __cinit__.
__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent, called upon object deallocation. It deallocates the C++ optimizer_params_t object pointed to by self.params, ensuring proper memory management.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: These properties provide controlled access to the various parameters of the encapsulated C++ optimizer_params_t object. Each property has a getter and a setter, allowing for both retrieval and modification of the optimizer's configuration.

listitem.para: @property Optimizer -> str: Gets and sets the name of the optimizer (e.g., "Adam", "SGD").
@property lr -> double: Gets and sets the learning rate for the optimizer.
@property lr_decay -> double: Gets and sets the learning rate decay factor.
@property weight_decay -> double: Gets and sets the weight decay (L2 penalty) for regularization.
@property initial_accumulator_value -> double: Gets and sets the initial value for the accumulators in optimizers like Adagrad.
@property eps -> double: Gets and sets a small constant for numerical stability (e.g., in Adam, Adagrad).
@property tolerance_grad -> double: Gets and sets the tolerance for the gradient in some optimizers.
@property tolerance_change -> double: Gets and sets the tolerance for change in some optimizers.
@property alpha -> double: Gets and sets the alpha parameter, often used in RMSprop.
@property momentum -> double: Gets and sets the momentum factor, used in optimizers like SGD with momentum.
@property dampening -> double: Gets and sets the dampening factor for momentum.
@property amsgrad -> bool: Gets and sets whether to use the AMSGrad variant of Adam.
@property centered -> bool: Gets and sets whether to use the centered RMSprop, which estimates the variance of the gradient.
@property nesterov -> bool: Gets and sets whether to use Nesterov momentum.
@property max_iter -> int: Gets and sets the maximum number of iterations for some optimizers.
@property max_eval -> int: Gets and sets the maximum number of function evaluations for some optimizers.
@property history_size -> int: Gets and sets the history size for optimizers like L-BFGS.
@property betas -> tuple: Gets and sets the beta parameters (e.g., (beta1, beta2) for Adam). The setter expects a Python tuple of two floats, which are then converted to a C++ vector<float>.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The OptimizerConfig class leverages Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class OptimizerConfig: Declares OptimizerConfig as a Cython extension type.
cdef optimizer_params_t *params: A C++ pointer to an optimizer_params_t object, managed by the Cython class.
from libcpp cimport float: Imports the C++ float type.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from tools.pxd, likely including env and enc for string conversions.
from AnalysisG.core.lossfx cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from lossfx.pxd, which defines optimizer_params_t.
env(self.params.optimizer) and enc(val): These functions handle the conversion between C++ std::string and Python str.
vector[float](val[0], val[1]): Demonstrates conversion of Python tuple to C++ std::vector<float>.
